Exclusive: Trump "close" to decision on "massive attack" on Iran
description: President Trump told Axios on Thursday that he is seriously considering restarting major combat operations in Iran — including strikes that would be bigger than the ones carried out during "Operation Epic Fury." Why it matters: In a brief interview, Trump acknowledged that such a decision would have consequences and stressed he hasn't made a determination yet. Trump didn't give a deadline for his decision. title: Why Iran is targeting Kuwait's desalination plants as part of its war strategy
description: Iranian strikes targeted Kuwait’s desalination and power plants for the fourth consecutive day on Monday. The attacks are putting unprecedented pressure on a Gulf country heavily dependant on desalination for its water supply. Iran is attempting to wage a “psychological war” on a country with few options for striking back, according to some analysts.

title: 'A recipe for disaster': France's dry southwest struggles to contain wildfire
description: A wildfire is moving extremely fast through a touristy peninsula close to the dune du Pila and Cap Ferret, dubbed 'the Hamptons of France'. Thousands of people have been forced to evacuate as firefighters struggle to contain the blaze. FRANCE 24's Christopher Bockman is in Toulouse and explains why the fire is spreading so fast.

title: Claims Swirl Around Possible Su-57 Crash near Moscow
description: A military aircraft crashed in Moscow’s suburbs, with unconfirmed reports identifying it as an Su-57 “Felon.” A military aircraft crashed near Moscow, in Russia, on Jul. 23, 2026. Russian press and defense commentators identified the aircraft as an Su-57 of the Russian Aerospace Forces (RuAF), although this was not yet officially confirmed at the time []

title: Greece to buy $4 billion air defense system from Israel
description: Greece has approved the purchase of a multi-layer air defense system worth up to €3.5 billion ($4 billion) from Israel.

title: Thousands evacuated due to wildfire in France's southwest
description: In France, while Paris enjoys respite from the record-breaking heat last month, the riviera and the southwest of France are being swept by fast-moving wildfires. Thousands of residents have been evacuated in a seaside holiday area.

title: Ghana’s president asks for AU debate on South Africa’s ‘escalating xenophobic attacks’
description: Ghanaian President John Mahama on Thursday called for an African Union (AU) debate to "formally address the escalating xenophobic attacks" on foreign nationals in South Africa. More than 160,000 foreign nationals have left South Africa and several have died over the past two months amid rising hostility against undocumented migrants.

title: Pentagon identifies third American soldier killed in Iranian attack in Jordan
description: Sgt. Angel S. Rampersad, 28, is believed to be the third active-duty soldier killed in action in an Iranian attack in Jordan, the DoD said.

title: Mountain biker becomes first to ride off a hot air balloon
description: You might want to look away from this next story if you're afraid of heights. A young Austrian mountain biker has performed a world first - by riding off a hot air balloon on his bike. Don't worry, both had parachutes attached to them. The trick, which took place in Bulgaria, took two years to prepare.

title: Middle East war's economic impact 'could get worse' if Houthis join in
description: US President Donald Trump threatened Yemen's Houthi rebels with "major military punishment" after they launched missile and drone strikes on oil tankers in the Red Sea. Concern has sparked across the globe regarding the rebels' involvement in the war. FRANCE 24's Kethevane Gorjestani tells us more.

title: The Bayeux Tapestry in London: 'A diplomatic operation that almost never happened'
description: Nearly 1,000 years after chronicling the Norman conquest of England, the Bayeux Tapestry is making history once again. The fragile 11th-century embroidery has arrived in London on an unprecedented loan to the British Museum, prompting delight and concern in equal measure. Celebrated as one of Europe''s greatest medieval treasures, its journey has reignited debates over heritage, diplomacy and the risks of moving delicate cultural artefacts.

title: After meeting with Moscow, Washington reiterates wish to mediate Ukraine war
description: On the sidelines of the ASEAN summit in Manila - the US and Russian foreign ministers met face-to-face. It's been more than a year since Marco Rubio and Sergei Lavrov last spoke. It won millions of followers on social media and tens of thousands of sign-ups since its launch in May.

title: US launches new round of strikes against Iran
description: The U.S. conducted a new wave of strikes against Iranian military sites on Wednesday after reimposing a naval blockade of the Islamic Republic's ports.

title: Ukrainians welcome new army chief but seek return of fired defence minister
description: Ukraine's soldiers and civilians welcomed the appointment of Mykhailo Drapaty as the new army chief on Wednesday, but many said they still wanted former defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov restored after his surprise removal sparked the largest war-related protests since Russia's 2022 invasion.

title: Trump threatens Iranian infrastructure over Strait of Hormuz attacks
description: US President Donald Trump warned on Wednesday that the US would destroy an Iranian bridge or power plant every time Tehran attacked shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. The threat came as Washington and Iran exchanged fresh strikes, regional missile alerts sounded and diplomatic efforts remained stalled.

title: US announces nuclear cooperation pact with Saudi Arabia
description: The United States on Wednesday announced that it had reached a civilian nuclear cooperation deal with Saudi Arabia, with Energy Secretary Chris Wright signing the pact and a separate bilateral safeguards deal alongside Saudi Energy Minister Prince Abdulaziz bin Salman. FRANCE 24's Fraser Jackson reports from Washington.

For decades, allegations of sexual exploitation in the fashion industry, spanning the globe, have resurfaced, often surrounding powerful figures whose deaths have complicated any semblance of accountability. title: Oil falls to 3-1/2-month low after US, Iran sign ceasefire deal
description:
BEIJING/SINGAPORE: Oil prices ​fell by more than $2 per barrel on Thursday after the US and Iran signed an interim agreement that ‌would end the Iran war, reopen the Strait of Hormuz and waive US sanctions on Tehran’s oil, boosting the oil supply outlook.

	Brent crude futures were down $2.14, or 2.69 percent, at $77.41 a barrel as of 09:16 a.m. Saudi time, and US West Texas Intermediate fell $2.36, or 3.07 percent, to $74.43 a barrel.

title: US will be candid in push for ‘hard-line’ NATO alliance, Hegseth says

title: French modelling scout linked to Epstein found dead
description: Daniel Siad, ⁠a modelling scout with ​ties to the late convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, has been ​found dead at his home outside Paris, prosecutors said on Wednesday. Siad's body was discovered on Monday evening. An autopsy will be carried out ​to ‌determine the cause of death, the Nanterre prosecutor's ⁠office said. Siad's name appeared nearly 2,000 times in the Epstein files released by the ‌U.S. Department of Justice. FRANCE 24's Emeral

title: Two foreign jihadists arrested in Nigeria for helping local terror groups
description: After 17 years, the Nigeria military’s efforts to contend a jihadist insurgency do not seem to be successful. The military is facing fresh hurdles with foreign fighters, including those from the Middle East joining the insurgents. The foreigners are bringing skills and technology that have proved very valuable in the Jihadists’ fight against the military. Details by FRANCE 24 correspondent in Lagos, Sam Olukoya.

At least 50 soldiers were killed in an ambush near the northern town of Anefis, in an attack claimed by Tuareg separatists and Al-Qaeda-linked jihadists. The assault is the latest in a string of setbacks, with militants also targeting Malian and Russian Africa Corps forces, downing a military helicopter, and briefly seizing territory. Details by Angela Diffley, FRANCE 24 international affairs editor.

title: The Iran war has cost the US $37.5 billion dollars, says Defense Secretary Pete Hesgseth
description: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth told a Senate committee that the Pentagon needs nearly 70 billion dollars in additional funding for the war in Iran. The hearing turned combative as Senators demanded explanations as to the military's strategy to win or otherwise end the war. Story by Bryan Quinn.

title: Germany greenlights controversial joint French-Russian nuclear project
description: Germany's environment ministry on Wednesday approved a French-Russian project to produce fuel rods used in Russian-designed reactors in the northwestern German town of Lingen. The joint venture project by a subsidiary of French company Framatome and Russia's state-owned Rosatom sparked fears of Russian spying, but German authorities said they had "no legal possibility to reject" it.

description:
WASHINGTON: Prominent US Senator Lindsey Graham, a key ally of President Donald Trump, died on Saturday aged 71 following a “brief and sudden illness,” his office said in a statement on his official X account.

title: Pete Hegseth asks US Senate for 'urgent' Iran war funding increase
description: The cost of the US military campaign against Iran has already cost 37.5 billion dollars. The figure was revealed by the US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth as he appeared before the Senate to make the case for the war and ask for an additional 67 billion dollars worth of funding. The move drew criticism from both Democrats and Republicans. Details and analysis by FRANCE 24 international affairs editor, Rochelle Ferguson-Bouyahi.

title: Zelensky dimisses military chief, accused of wasting lives by demonstrators
description: It is the biggest shake up in Ukraine's military leadership since Russia's full scale invasion of Ukraine – a shake up designed to end what has also been one of the country's biggest political crises since the invasion. After days of street protests over last week's removal of the defence minister, President Volodymyr Zelensky sacked the military's commander in chief. Will the change be enough to placate the protesters? Answer with FRANCE 24 corr

title: Louvre Museum's Apollo gallery reopens, almost a year after the heist
description: It is the gallery from where 88 million euros worth of France's crown jewels were stolen last year in a dramatic heist. Thieves disguised as construction workers and broke into the gallery, using a truck mounted lift. Today, the Apollo Gallery of Paris's Louvre Museum is to reopen to the public. No jewels will be on display in the room – it will revert to its 17th century function as a state gallery. Details by FRANCE 24 journalist Carys Garland.

title: US eyes strikes on Iran's Pickaxe Mountain, believed to be an enrichment site
description: Donald Trump has said that Pickaxe Mountain in Iran could be bombed imminently by the US. The fortified site, buried deep underground, is believed to be an enrichment facility. Story by Ellen Gainsford.

title: 🔴 Daniel Siad, French modelling scout linked to US sex offender Epstein, found dead outside Paris
French modelling scout linked to US sex offender Epstein found dead outside Paris
description: Daniel Siad, a French modelling scout with links to sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, was found dead at his home outside Paris, prosecutors said on Wednesday morning.
